name: we-were-soldiers-once
description: Lt. Gen. Harold G. Moore and Joseph L. Galloway's classic account of the Battle of Ia Drang in November 1965 — the first major engagement between the U.S. Army and the North Vietnamese Army, a brutal four-day fight that set the pattern for the entire Vietnam War.
useCase: Understanding the Vietnam War's first major battle, airmobile warfare tactics, small-unit leadership under extreme stress, the cost of war, and the brotherhood of combat soldiers.
knownRisks: Extremely graphic combat descriptions — may be disturbing
The book has been criticized for minimizing some controversial aspects of the battle (friendly fire, the fate of the lost platoon)
Written from the American perspective primarily, though it respects the NVA soldiers
Some details have been disputed by other participants
